title: Novel oil spill indices for sentinel-2 imagery: A case study of natural seepage in Qaruh Island, Kuwait
author: Zakzouk, El-Magd, et al.
materials: (iv) Visualization and contrast assessment of band combinations:
Σ1,Σ2 are covariance matrices of the two classes.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Utilizing the Sentinel Hub Earth Observation browser (website link: https://www.sentinel-hub.com/explore/eobrowser), all available scenes from Landsat 8, Sentinel-1, and Sentinel-2 that covered Qaruh Island between 2017 and 2022 have been visually checked. From this extensive dataset, we specifically selected and downloaded instances of the most significant oil spills via GEE to help better investigate the spectral properties of oil properties in the marine environment.(ii)Ground truth preparation:

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: For the chosen Sentinel-2 scenes, oil spill ground truth masks have been established to be used in later results assessment. Masking was accomplished using the Q-GIS software (version 3.22.4), relying on digitization complemented by adaptive thresholding techniques. This method's efficacy is contingent upon the image quality and the discernibility of oil spills from the surrounding waters.(iii)Spectral profile analysis:

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: The spectral profiles of oil and water pixels that resided within the data interquartile range (IQR) have been plotted. By emphasizing the IQR, the analysis becomes less susceptible to extreme values, effectively minimizing the impact of potential outliers.(iv)Band Combination Exploration:

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Inspired by the literature, all conceivable band combinations of the mathematical formulations presented in Table 3 have been generated for evaluation. The current study sought to exhaustively test every combination derived from these foundational formulas.Table 3Proposed mathematical formulas for generating band combinations.Table 3Naming ConventionBand RatioConsiderationAvailable CombinationsRatio 1BandA−BandBBandA+BandBBand A > Band B66Ratio 2BandABandBBand A > Band B66Ratio 3BandA+BandBBandCBand A ≠ Band B ≠ Band C220Ratio 4BandA+BandBBandC+BandDBand A ≠ Band B ≠ Band C ≠ Band D495Generated Combinations859

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: To assess the effectiveness of generated band ratios for better distinguishing between oil spills and surrounding water in Sentinel-2 spectral bands, the Jeffries-Matusita (JM) distance statistical metric has been employed [42]. This measure serves as a robust tool to quantify the separability between two distinct classes within a feature space. Within the context of remote sensing, this measure is often used to determine the best spectral bands or band ratios for distinguishing between two land cover classes [43]. The JM distance operates on a scale ranging from 0 to 2 (approximately 1.4142). A value of 0 signifies a complete overlap between the classes, while a score of approximately 1.4142 denotes absolute separability.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: The JM distance between two classes A and B is calculated as in Eq. (1):(1)JM=2(1−e−B)Where the Bhattacharyya distance for two classes is given by Eq. (2):(2)B=18(μ1−μ2)T∑(μ1−μ2)−1+12ln(det(Σ)det(Σ1)×det(Σ2))Where: μ1,μ2 are the mean vectors of the two classes; Σis the average covariance matrix

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Also, visual inspection and interpretation as one of the simplest ways to judge the best band ratio based on contrast between the oil spill and the surrounding water has been combined in analysis.(v)Selection and assessment of optimal band combinations:

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Upon computing JM distances, the most effective band combination have been identified. This optimal combination was then integrated into three other channel combinations. Subsequently, the JM distances for these multiband images have been recalculated, ensuring a comprehensive evaluation to ultimately pinpoint the premier multiband combination.(vi)Unsupervised clustering for accuracy assessment:

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: For the distinguished multiband combination, two-class unsupervised clustering approaches have been used. These served as an additional metric to gauge the accuracy of developed indices in comparison to previously established ones.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Data classification without prior knowledge of the categories relies on inherent data properties, and group similar data pixels. The K-means and the Gaussian Mixture Model Algorithms were employed in this regard. The strength of Two-Class unsupervised clustering lies in its capacity to rapidly discern contrasting features within a dataset, making it particularly useful selecting best band combinations.(a)K-means clustering

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: It aims to partition n_samples into n_clusters in which each sample belongs to the cluster whose mean is closest to it [44]. The objective function that K-means minimizes is the within-cluster sum of squares as given by Eq. (3):(3)J=∑i=1kΣμj∈Ci(∥xj−μi∥2)Where: J represents the total within-cluster variance; K is the number of desired clusters; Ci denotes the ith cluster; μjis the ith cluster center; xi is a data point belonging to cluster Ci; ∥.∥ is the Euclidean distance(b)Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM):

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: GMM is a probabilistic model that assumes that the data is generated from a mixture of several Gaussian distributions [45]. Each of these Gaussian distributions represents a cluster. Hence, a GMM tries to fit these Gaussian distributions to the data.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Given a dataset, the objective is to maximize the likelihood of the parameters of the model, given the data. If we have K Gaussian components, the likelihood for the entire dataset X={x1,x2,….xN} is given by Eqs. (4) and (5):(4)L(θ|X)=∑i=1KπkN(xi|μk,∑k)(5)N(xi|μk,∑k)=1(2π)d/2|Σ|1/2exp(−12(x−μ)TΣ−1(x−μ))Where: x is the data point; k is the number of Gaussian distributions; πk are the mixture coefficients that represent the probability that a randomly selected x originated from component k.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: N(xi|μk,∑k) is the Gaussian distribution function with mean μkand covariance ∑k and d is the dimensionality of the data

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: θ represent represents the parameters of the model (i.e., the means μk, covariances ∑k, and mixture coefficients μk of each Gaussian component).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: It models data as being generated from a mixture of several Gaussian distributions, and then assigns each data point to one of these distributions.(c)Accuracy Assessment

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Accuracy assessment of clustered classes has been done to ensure the reliability and credibility of proposed indices. Key metrics used in this assessment include accuracy, sensitivity, precision and F1 scores. These metrics provide insights into the proportion of correctly classified pixels, the likelihood of misclassification, and the agreement between the mask and the reference data beyond random chance as follow:

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: True Positives (tp): The number of positive instances (labeled as 1) that were correctly classified as positive by the model. tp=∑(mask=1∧truth=1)

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: False Negatives (fn): The number of positive instances that were incorrectly classified as negative (0) by the model. fn=∑(mask=0∧truth=1)

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: False Positives (fp): The number of negative instances (labeled as 0) that were incorrectly classified as positive by the model. fp=∑(mask=1∧truth=1)

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: True Negatives (tn): The number of negative instances that were correctly classified as negative by the model. tn=∑(mask=0∧truth=0)

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Sensitivity: The proportion of actual positive instances that were correctly classified. It shows how well the model can detect positive instances. Sensitivity=tptp+fn

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Precision: The proportion of positive identifications by the model that were actually correct. It shows how many of the instances that the model labeled as positive are indeed positive. Precision=tptp+fp

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: F1 Score: The harmonic mean of precision and sensitivity. It is a balance between precision and sensitivity. F1=2×tp2×tp+fp+fn

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Accuracy: The proportion of all instances that were correctly classified, both positive and negative. Accuracy=tp+tntp+tn+fp+fn.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: A simplified illustration of the sequence of analysis is illustrated in Fig. 2.Fig. 2Flowchart depicting the algorithm for developing and assessing the best oil spill index.Fig 2

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: In the course of analysis, several Python libraries have been employed to facilitate data processing, visualization, and statistical analysis. The following provides a comprehensive list of these libraries:-Basic utilities and file handling: os, shutil, and csv for general file operations and directory management.-Raster data processing: The rasterio library was instrumental in handling raster datasets, while rasterio.warp was used for reprojecting and resampling raster data.-Data manipulation and analysis: pandas and numpy, and statistical functions were derived from scipy.stats and scipy.linalg.-Visualization: Graphical representations were crafted using matplotlib.pyplot, with seaborn enhancing the visual aesthetics. The matplotlib.colors module provided additional color normalization functionalities.-Machine Learning and Data Preprocessing: The sklearn library was extensively used, with modules such as sklearn.preprocessing for data scaling, sklearn.cluster for clustering algorithms like KMeans, and sklearn.mixture for Gaussian Mixture models. Model evaluation metrics like accuracy, recall, precision, and F1 score were sourced from sklearn.metrics.-Miscellaneous Utilities: itertools for creating combinations, re for regular expression operations, textwrap for text formatting, and datetime for handling date and time operations.
